zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir George Preston I Laird of Craigmillar於大約1366年出生在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安可萊格米勒 卡素時，他的父親Sir Simon Preston II28歲，母親Mrs Simon Preston24歲。他和Eleanor Annabella De Farnelleis至少有2個兒子。他去世於1424年8月在英國蘇格蘭艾丁波可萊格米拉可萊格米拉 卡素 帕克 其特利享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: Preston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from one of the many places (most notably one in Lancashire) so called from Old English prēost ‘priest’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
occasionally from Middle English prest + son ‘son of the priest’. Priests were forbidden marriage but many produced illegitimate offspring.

variants.name: Presson
meanings: English (Cheshire and Lancashire): variant of   Preston  .
French: occupational name for a presser of wine or oil, from a derivative of presser ‘to press’.

variants.name: Prestwich
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from a place in Lancashire (now Greater Manchester), so called from Old English prēost ‘priest’ + wīc ‘outlying settlement’, or from other places with the same derivation. Variants such as Prestidge, Prestage, etc., parallel the local pronunciation of the Lancashire name. Compare Preston .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir George Preston I Laird of Craigmillar was born about 1366, in Craigmiller Castle, Midlothian, Scotland, his father, Sir Simon Preston II, was 28 and his mother, Mrs Simon Preston, was 24. He had at least 2 sons with Eleanor Annabella De Farnelleis. He died in August 1424, in Craigmillar Castle Park Cemetery, Craigmillar, Edinburgh, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.

Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Eleanor Annabella De Farnelleis. Il est décédé en août 1424, à Craigmillar Castle Park Cemetery, Craigmillar, Edinburgh, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans.
